message.abstract:
<jats:title>Abstract</jats:title>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Introduction</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>In Brazil, practices of medical students have been interrupted due to COVID-19 to meet emergency demands.</jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Aim</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>To describe a strategy for a controlled return to the presential practices for medical students.</jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Methods</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>We developed a standardized protocol to be applied before and during the return of the practical classes in medical students of Universidade Federal Fluminense, in the follow months after COVID-19 pandemic beginning. The protocol was comprised in three parts: Remote training on COVID-19 prevention; Face-to-face training focused in COVID-19 prevention, handwashing and personal protective equipment use; Investigation of students COVID-19 status before starting practical activities and weekly monitoring for COVID-19 during seven weeks. The training was done by medical teachers in small groups for medical students of the last lective semester.</jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Results</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>The classes were interrupted on March 12, 2020 and returned in August 10, 2020. Seventy-one students were trained and followed. The mean age was 26.6 years (26.7 ±0.835) and 54% were female. Forty-nine (69%) students over 71 had a private health insurance, 60 (84.5%) shared a house/apartment with one or more person and 12(16.9%) reported a previous comorbidity. Eighteen (25.4%) over 71 reported previous symptoms of COVID-19, being positive in two students. During the follow-up, fourteen (19.7%) over 71 students were placed in quarantine due to signals/symptoms compatible with COVID-19 or contact with symptomatic case. Only two cases (2.8%) were confirmed and occurred in Brazilian epidemiological week 37.</jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Conclusion</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>The protocol was successful in minimizing COVID-19 acquisition during practical classes of medical students.</jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
title: Back to the medical classes-Part I-Strategy for return to the presential practices during COVID-19 pandemics in a Brazilian Medical School

reference.unstructured: Cenário Epidemiológico da COVID-19 no Estado do Rio de Janeiro-Julho 2021-Governo do Estado do Rio de Janeiro. Available at: https://painel.saude.rj.gov.br/arquivos/Cenario_Covid19_2021_2307.pdf. Accessed on September 10, 2021
